sections.subtitle: Team Form
sections.text: Atlético Madrid come into this match with a solid run, securing 2 wins, 2 draws, and 1 loss in their last 5 matches, showcasing resilience despite a heavy 4-0 defeat to Arsenal. Sevilla, on the other hand, have a mixed record with 3 wins and 2 losses in their last 5, but their defensive vulnerabilities are evident with no clean sheets in their last 4 games. Atlético's consistency and home advantage at Riyadh Air Metropolitano give them an edge.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Atlético Madrid have dominated Sevilla, winning both matches (4-3 at home and 2-1 away). These high-scoring games suggest both teams tend to find the net, with 7 goals in their most recent clash, but Atlético's ability to edge out close results stands out.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Atlético Madrid, sitting 4th in the league with 19 points, boast a strong attack (18 goals, 3rd in La Liga) and a tight defense (10 goals conceded, 2nd best). Sevilla, positioned 11th with 13 points, have scored 17 goals but struggle defensively, conceding 16. Atlético's superior ball possession (53.7%) and final third entries (31.4 per game) highlight their control in matches.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: Julián Alvarez has been pivotal for Atlético, with 6 goals this season (2nd in La Liga) and a high Sofascore rating of 7.22, posing a constant threat. Sevilla will need to contain him while relying on their attacking moments, though their lack of standout in-form players from the last 5 matches could hinder them.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Atlético Madrid's strong home form, head-to-head dominance, and Sevilla's defensive struggles, a home win seems likely, aligning with Sofascore's 67% probability. Expect a competitive match, but Atlético should capitalize on their attacking prowess and defensive solidity to secure the victory.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Atlético Madrid and Sevilla at the Riyadh Air Metropolitano ended in a goalless draw. Neither team managed to break the deadlock, but the stats and play indicate a clear disparity in attacking intent and control. Atlético Madrid showed more dominance in key areas, particularly in their ability to create opportunities and penetrate the opposition's defense. Sevilla, on the other hand, struggled to make a significant impact in the final third, often being pushed back by a well-organized home defense.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Home team performance
halftimeSections.text: Atlético Madrid had a promising first half, showcasing their attacking intent with several notable chances created. They dominated in terms of entries into the final third and penalty area, indicating their ability to build pressure consistently. Despite missing some big opportunities, their overall play was fluid, with players like Alejandro Baena standing out for their creativity in midfield. Their high percentage of duels won, especially in the air, further underlines their physical dominance over Sevilla.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Away team performance
halftimeSections.text: Sevilla had a less convincing first half, struggling to create meaningful chances. Their limited entries into the final third and penalty area reflect a lack of attacking cohesion. Defensively, they managed to hold firm against Atlético's pressure, but their low percentage of duels won suggests they were often second to the ball. Their possession was marginally higher, yet it lacked purpose, often being contained in less dangerous areas of the pitch.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The game started with Sevilla showing early intent, pushing forward in the opening minutes with consistent attacking spells. However, the momentum shifted significantly around the midway point of the first half as Atlético Madrid took control, launching a series of sustained attacks and maintaining pressure for long stretches. Towards the latter part of the half, there were brief moments where Sevilla regained some initiative, but Atlético quickly reasserted themselves with dangerous moves before the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Atlético Madrid, Alejandro Baena has been pivotal with his vision and ability to create chances, making him a key figure to watch in the second half. Alexander Sørloth also poses a constant threat up front with his positioning and involvement in the attack. On the Sevilla side, Isaac Romero will need to step up if they are to turn things around, though he has been relatively quiet so far. Their defense, marshaled by players like Marcão, will also be crucial in containing Atlético's persistent attacks.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Given the first half dynamics, Atlético Madrid are likely to continue their aggressive approach in the second half, looking to convert their dominance into goals. Sevilla will need to find a way to disrupt Atlético's rhythm, possibly through quicker transitions or tactical adjustments to exploit any defensive lapses. If Atlético maintain their intensity, they are the favorites to break the deadlock. However, Sevilla could pose a threat on the counter if they manage to stabilize their midfield play.
